title: Fix archival doc-reference integrity: auto-relink on archive + doc-ref-canonical plan resolution
description:
Archival of epic 25064508 left the repository with dangling document references that make `jit validate` hard-fail, blocking all code-review gates (which require validate to pass). Discovered while driving the code-smell-cleanup epic; the archival UX is itself a quality defect.

Two distinct failure modes were observed:
1. **Move-without-relink**: the planning node `0d753ba5`'s plan doc was moved to `dev/archive/features/25064508/plan.md`, but its doc reference (and validate's resolution) still point at the dead `dev/active/25064508-d563-4073-a970-296607a01adc-plan.md`. Validate resolves the plan-doc location from the global *template* `dev/active/{container.id}-plan.md`, not from the planning node's reference — a dual source of truth that drifts on archival.
2. **Delete-while-referenced**: `dev/active/9ac9fdac-dogfood-25064508.md` was deleted by archival commit `380f40c2` but is still referenced (label "Dogfood evidence") by two done issues. It is recoverable from git history (created in `9c42f647`).

## Scope

Three parts, one worktree, gated and merged:

1. **Repair the current dangling refs** so `jit validate` exits 0: re-link `0d753ba5`'s plan reference to the archived plan path, and restore the dogfood evidence file from git history into the archive (`dev/archive/features/25064508/`) and re-link the two referencing issues to it.
2. **Archive auto-relink + integrity** (per-document `jit doc archive`): the archive operation must, atomically, relocate the file AND update every issue doc-reference that points to it to the new archive path. If the target file is missing, it must fail with a typed error and make NO changes (never leave or create a dangling reference).
3. **Doc-ref-canonical plan resolution**: validate must resolve a breakable container's plan-doc location from the planning node's recorded plan doc-reference (the graph template provides only the creation-time default). A plan that is moved/archived and re-linked then keeps validate green with no leniency; a genuinely missing plan while the planning node is `done` still errors.

This touches `commands/validate.rs`, `commands/document.rs`, and `commands/plan_doc.rs` — areas another session (epic 52ad3b1f) is also editing; implement in an isolated worktree and merge carefully.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `jit validate` exits 0 on the repository (no dangling document references); the two known dangling refs (25064508 plan, 9ac9fdac dogfood) are repaired with the files at their final archived paths.
- [ ] `jit doc archive <path>` is transactional and reference-aware: it relocates the file to the archive root AND re-links every issue doc-reference pointing at it to the new path in one operation; it fails with a typed `thiserror` error and makes no filesystem or `.jit` changes when the source file is missing. Covered by tests (happy path re-links N references; missing-file path is a no-op error).
- [ ] Validate resolves a breakable container's plan-doc location from the planning node's plan doc-reference, not the hardcoded `dev/active/{id}` template; the template is used only as the default at `jit apply plan` time. Covered by a test: a plan moved to a new path + re-linked keeps validate green; a missing plan with a `done` planning node still errors.
- [ ] No reliance on lifecycle leniency: dangling references remain a hard validate error in all states (per project decision).
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` gates pass; existing bracket/validate tests still pass.
